setting.note: A house on the free side of the Ohio River, haunted by a mother's choice under slavery. The map runs from the Kentucky plantation of the past, across the river into Ohio, out to a house and a clearing on the northern edge of Cincinnati, and back to the downtown riverfront where the real case behind the novel unfolded.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Toni Morrison, first published 1987. This book is mapped as a cited work: we store none of its text and reproduce no passages. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: Beloved, Toni Morrison (1987). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: A deep oxblood red for a book that turns on blood and a mother's grief. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
